name: The Brew Nook
description: A low wooden counter serves as the heart of this stall, where steam rises in lazy violet curls from ceramic pots. Tall stools are occupied by weary artisans speaking in low, modulated tones. A chalkboard lists beverages by their viscosity rather than their flavor. The floor is covered in dried reeds that muffle the noise of the main market.
secret: The reeds on the floor conceal a loose floorboard where vendors hide 'grey market' items for quick hand-offs. The tea's light frequency can be used to reveal certain invisible inks found on market ledgers.
scenery: A chalkboard listing prices for 'Dense Violet', 'Sheer Amber', and 'Opaque Grey'.
A row of mismatched ceramic mugs with thumb-rests shaped for different hand structures.
A small stove made of blue tile that hums at a low frequency.

ai.prompt: The atmosphere is hushed and contemplative. Focus on the sensory details of the strange liquids and the quiet commerce of information.
ai.conversationPrompt: The patrons here are mostly off-duty market vendors discussing the fluctuating price of raw materials and market gossip. They are polite but tired.

name: Food Row
description: A stretch of the market given over to food stalls. Steam rises from griddles and pots. A vendor fries something flat and round on a stone heated from beneath. Another serves bowls of broth from a vat tall enough to stand in. The smells change every few steps. A bench along one wall is occupied by vendors on their breaks, eating quickly.
secret: Food Row serves as the market's informal commons — vendors from different sections come here to eat and gossip. Conversations overheard here hint at what's selling, what's scarce, and who's been buying in bulk. The broth vendor, who has been here longer than anyone, knows every regular by name and order.

name: The Brew Nook Entrance
description: A quieter spot further along, where steam rises from cups that glow faintly.

name: Food Row
description: Leads back to Food Row.

name: Koro
description: Koro is a vendor with four slender arms and skin the color of wet slate. They move with an efficiency that suggests they have been pouring tea for several centuries. They don't look up from the pot they are stirring, but they acknowledge your presence with a slight tilt of their head. Koro is known to accept secrets as a valid currency for the high-end infusions.
secret: Koro is looking for 'stasis gel' to preserve a particularly volatile bloom they want to brew. If offered information about where to find rare materials, they will provide a 'Tears of Light' brew for free.

name: Mug of Neon Infusion
description: A heavy ceramic mug containing a liquid that pulses with a rhythmic yellow light. It feels warmer than the surrounding air but doesn't emit any steam. The light flickers slightly when you speak near it.
secret: Drinking this causes the player's skin to glow faintly for a short duration, effectively acting as a light source in dark rooms. The effect is triggered by ingestion.

name: Lithic Shaper Passage
description: The purple steam of the brew shops thins, replaced by a crisp draft that smells of wet slate and precision. Tall, narrow walls of roughly hewn basalt press inward, their surfaces covered in fine white dust from the workshops ahead. A gutter runs down the center of the path, carrying a slow-moving sludge of gray particulate. This is a quiet transition, according to those who find the sound of chisels more relaxing than conversation.
secret: The white dust on the walls is actually a byproduct of 'ghost-rock' carving; it glows faintly in total darkness. If the player smears the dust on their eyes, they gain temporary thermal vision.
scenery: A steady trickle of gray runoff in the center gutter.
Basalt walls etched with geometric testing patterns.
A discarded sanding block made of rough volcanic glass.

ai.prompt: The atmosphere is industrial but subdued. Focus on the physical labor of stone-shaping and the properties of different mineral dusts.

name: Steam-Fogged Alleyway
description: A narrow gap between the tea stall and a masonry workshop, heavy with condensation.

name: The Brew Nook
description: Leads back to The Brew Nook.

name: magnetic grit bin
description: A heavy iron bin filled with fine black sand that shivers and peaks when metal is brought near it. A small wooden scoop hangs from a chain on the side. The sand is used for polishing surfaces that are too hard for standard abrasives.
secret: If the grit is combined with a liquid, it creates a viscous paste that can be moved by waving a metal object over it. It can also detect hidden magnetic fields in nearby walls or floors.

name: petrified chisel
description: A tool that appears to have been carved from a single piece of dark, fossilized wood. It is surprisingly heavy and the edge is sharper than the steel tools found in other sectors. It feels cold to the touch, regardless of the ambient temperature.
secret: This chisel does not dull when used on stone, but it will shatter instantly if it strikes living wood. It is particularly effective at carving 'singing' stones without dampening their resonance.

name: The Dust-Mason Ateliers
description: A row of low-slung, open-fronted sheds lines this wide stone terrace, each echoing with the rhythmic tap-tap of hammers on granite. Thick slabs of variegated marble and porous pumice are stacked like cordwood between the workbenches. The air is so saturated with mineral dust that it feels heavy, providing a tactile quality to the silence between strikes. The masons here are very busy, which is why they haven't noticed you are standing in their light.
secret: The dust in the air is actually a mix of different stone types; if the player has a magnetic item, they can pull specific metallic filings out of the air to collect them as raw material.
scenery: A stack of blue-veined marble slabs leaning against a pillar.
A heavy wooden workbench scarred by decades of chisel slips.
A bucket of grey sludge used for cooling cutting blades.

ai.prompt: The atmosphere is industrial and productive. Vendors here focus on stone types, weights, and masonry tools. The masons are indifferent to travelers unless they have interesting materials to shape.

name: The Chisel-Shed District Entrance
description: The passage opens into a wider area where the rhythmic tapping of hammers against stone becomes audible.

name: Lithic Shaper Passage
description: The path leads back through the narrow basalt walls toward the brew shops.

name: vibrating tuning fork
description: A heavy bronze fork that hums with a low, constant frequency. When held against a block of stone, the vibrations change pitch based on the density of the material.
secret: If used on 'singing' stones or resonant minerals, it can find structural flaws or hidden cavities. If struck against a petrified chisel, it produces a harmonic that can briefly soften solid stone like clay.

name: pumice slurry vat
description: A stone basin filled with a thick, frothy gray liquid. It looks like bubbling mud, but it is actually a suspension of ultra-fine abrasive minerals used for polishing.

properties.takeRefusal: The slurry is too heavy and messy to carry without a proper container.

name: Hydro-Abrasive Gallery
description: A constant hiss dominates this narrow corridor where needle-thin jets of water pierce the silence. Massive slabs of igneous rock sit on sliding steel rails, slowly creeping through the path of the cutters to be bisected with surgical precision. The floor is perpetually slick with a slurry of water and garnet sand, which the masons seem to consider a decorative choice.
secret: The floor drains contain large amounts of settled garnet sand. If a player searches the drains, they can recover high-quality abrasive material for crafting or for use with the lance.
scenery: The smell of wet stone and ozone.
Thin, vibrating lines of water that look solid until they hit stone.
Piles of garnet sand in ceramic bins.

ai.prompt: The environment is loud and wet. Masons here are technical and precise, more like engineers than artists. The water pressure is lethal if touched directly.

name: High-Pressure Cutters
description: A narrow alley where the sound of masonry changes from rhythmic tapping to a high-pitched, continuous scream.

name: The Dust-Mason Ateliers
description: Leads back to The Dust-Mason Ateliers.

name: Heavy Hoist Arch
description: A wide, reinforced archway where massive stones are winched toward the polishing yards.

exit.destinationIntent: A sprawling outdoor yard where stones are organized and polished using large-scale machinery.

name: handheld abrasive lance
description: A heavy tool connected to a thick, reinforced hose. It features a brass nozzle that feels cold from the high-velocity flow inside, and a safety lever that looks like it requires a firm grip. The nozzle is caked with dried garnet grit.
secret: If the player uses this on a hard material while carrying magnetic grit, the grit will be sucked into the stream, allowing the lance to cut through nearly anything. Without an abrasive additive, it just washes things very aggressively.

name: Senior Cutter Vrell
description: A mason wearing thick rubberized aprons and a glass visor that is constantly being wiped clean. They are monitoring a pressure gauge with the intensity of a surgeon, occasionally tapping the dial with a blue-skinned knuckle. They don't look up, as looking up is how you lose an eye to a stray spray.

ai.conversationPrompt: Vrell is a perfectionist who views stone as a series of math problems. They are willing to discuss stone densities and the proper grit-to-water ratio for cutting obsidian.

name: Notice Board
description: A weathered wooden board bristling with pinned papers. Requests for rare materials, offers of services, warnings about unreliable vendors, a few sketched maps of market sections. Most are in scripts you can't read, but some have been helpfully illustrated. One recurring notice, pinned in several places, reads: BRING FINISHED GOODS TO THE COLLECTOR — FAIR PRICES PAID.
secret: Some of the notices are written in a script that changes when you're not looking directly at it. One particular notice, pinned under several layers of others, is a map of the market that shows stalls and passages that don't seem to exist yet — or don't exist anymore.

scenery.description: The maps are hand-drawn on scraps of vellum and dried leaf, showing the market as a shifting cluster of circles and overlapping planes. One particular map depicts a series of back-alleys that appear to connect the nut vendor's cart directly to a distant forge. The ink on that one seems to migrate across the page if you blink too fast.
scenery.rejection: The map is pinned firmly behind several layers of more recent commerce.

properties.takeRefusal: The board is bolted to the wall.

name: The Fair Exchange
description: An open court beyond Oddments Alley, paved in stone that's been swept clean. The stalls here are more permanent — wooden frames with cloth walls, some with actual doors. A sign at the entrance reads ALL PRICES POSTED in letters that look like they mean it. The vendors sit behind their counters with the patient attention of people who know exactly what they want and are prepared to wait for it.
secret: The prices are posted, but not in money. One stall's sign reads: "One hour of your undivided attention." Another: "A favor, to be named." The signs are binding — not magically, but by the market's custom, which here amounts to the same thing. Vendors who don't honor posted terms lose their pitch.
